name: Dick Sudhalter
abstract: Richard Merrill Sudhalter (28 December 1938 – 19 September 2008) was an American jazz trumpeter and writer.

concerts.label: New York Jazz Repertory Company: The Music of Bix Beiderbecke

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: And the trumpets were, on that first album, Paulie Cohen, Laurie Frink, Dick Sudhalter and John Eckert.
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: You know Dick Sudhalter? ... Dick Sudhalter new book on Hoagy Carmichael. The music illustrations Justin copied for him. So I mean he’s doing — right now he’s copying some symphony stuff for me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: George Masso
about_quotes.evidence: Dick Sudhalter was leading the band... we try it with the fermata... we just played that the way he felt like.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Masso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Spiegle Willcox and Al Gallodoro
about_quotes.evidence: Dick Sudhalter had borrowed some of the music from Williams College... all of a sudden I seen my handwriting
about_quotes.doc_title: Spiegle Willcox and Al Gallodoro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Norwich, New York, November 14, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Crow
about_quotes.evidence: Dick Sudhalter was one of his writers. So when he asked these... they both pointed at me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Crow interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995
